name: Lab 2 — Active Directory Attack & Defense
description: CyberConstruct SOC Lab — soc.local AD attack chain simulation. Techniques executed, detected in ELK SIEM, and hardened. Analyst: John Medina.

techniques.comment: EXECUTED & DETECTED — BloodHound/SharpHound LDAP enumeration of soc.local. Collected 315 objects. Detected via LDAP query burst (Event 1644). Hardened: DS Access auditing enabled.

EXECUTED & DETECTED — Kerberoasted svc-sql-report (SPN: MSSQLSvc/win-dc01.soc.local:1433) using Impacket GetUserSPNs. RC4 hash captured. Detected via Event 4769 etype 0x17 (237 events in ELK). Hardened: RC4 disabled, AES128+AES256 enforced on svc-sql-report.

EXECUTED & DETECTED — AS-REP Roasted svc-backup-ops (DoesNotRequirePreAuth=True) using Impacket GetNPUsers. AS-REP hash captured. Detected via Event 4768 no pre-auth (98 events in ELK). Hardened: Kerberos pre-authentication re-enabled on svc-backup-ops.

NOT EXECUTED — Detection gap identified. Primary detection signal: Event 4769 with anomalous TGS ticket lifetime. Recommendation: rotate krbtgt password twice, deploy MDI for behavioral Golden Ticket detection.

ATTEMPTED & BLOCKED — LSASS dump attempted via Mimikatz/ProcDump from WIN10-ADCLIENT. Blocked by Windows Defender Protected Process Light (PPL). Sysmon Event 10 generated and detected in ELK. Documented as defensive control validation.

EXECUTED & DETECTED — DCSync executed from Kali-Attacker using Impacket secretsdump. NTLM hashes replicated from WIN-DC01 including krbtgt. Detected via Event 4662 DS-Replication-Get-Changes GUIDs (103 events in ELK). Hardened: DS Access auditing + Protected Users group for privileged accounts.

RESIDUAL RISK — svc-sql-report retains SQLAdmin privileges on WIN-DC01. Post-hardening BloodHound path: svc-sql-report → SQLAdmin → WIN-DC01 → HasSession → r.hayes → Domain Admins. Recommendation: remove SQLAdmin rights from service account (least privilege).
DETECTED — Pass-the-Hash lateral movement detected via Event 4624 LogonType 3 with NTLM authentication. Detection rule validated in ELK.
